steps.title: Find the axis (pencil)
steps.description: Lightly sketch a vertical line down the center of where the pot will sit — this axis keeps both sides of the pot balanced as you build it.

steps.title: Block the pot (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, sketch a simple trapezoid around the axis — wider at the top, narrower at the base. Add a shallow ellipse along the top rim to show that you're looking slightly down into the pot.

steps.title: Rough in the foliage (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, sketch a loose cloud-shape above the pot for the overall mass of leaves. Let it lean a little to one side — herbs rarely grow perfectly symmetric.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the pot
steps.description: Switch to ink. Trace the pot's outline and the front of the rim ellipse with confident strokes. Stop the back of the rim where the leaves will cover it — no need to draw lines that won't be seen.

steps.title: Ink the leaves (ink)
steps.description: Still in ink, draw a handful of small leaf shapes inside the foliage cloud — short tapered marks branching out from a few implied stems. Don't draw every leaf; suggest the bushiness with maybe a dozen marks.

steps.title: Add a small detail (ink)
steps.description: Add a single horizontal line across the pot just under the rim to suggest a band or lip. One mark is enough to make the pot feel ceramic rather than blank.
